365→// SECTION 9: COROUTINE INITIALIZATION (set up initial stack frame)
   366→// =============================================================================
   367→
   368→// Global bootstrap pointer — set before first context switch into a coro.
   369→// Safe because coroutine switches are cooperative (single-threaded).
   370→tlocal StackCtxBootstrap* g_stack_ctx_bootstrap;
   371→
   372→/**
   373→ * Trampoline that runs when a coroutine starts for the first time.
   374→ *
   375→ * This is a normal C3 function (not naked), so it can call arbitrary code.
   376→ * It reads the bootstrap info from the thread-local, calls the entry function,
   377→ * then marks the coroutine as completed and switches back to the parent.
   378→ */
   379→fn void stack_ctx_trampoline() {
   380→    StackCtxBootstrap* boot = g_stack_ctx_bootstrap;
   381→    StackCtxEntryFn entry = boot.entry;
   382→    void* arg = boot.arg;
   383→
   384→    // Set current coro from bootstrap (only valid for first switch)
   385→    g_current_stack_ctx = boot.self;
   386→
   387→    // Call the actual entry function
   388→    entry(arg);
   389→

521→
   522→    g_current_stack_ctx = saved;
   523→}
   524→
   525→// Also update stack_ctx_switch_to to track current coro
   526→// (done inline below in the existing stack_ctx_switch_to — we add tracking)
   527→
   528→// =============================================================================
   529→// SECTION 10: STACK CLONING (for multi-shot continuations)
   530→// =============================================================================
